decks.notes: This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that can also take advantage of blinking effects to disrupt opponents and gain value. It includes a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and blink spells to keep the pressure on the opponent while also controlling the board. By using blink effects to repeatedly trigger enter-the-battlefield abilities of creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Quantum Riddler, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and create difficult-to-answer threats. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Overlord of the Balemurk to close out games quickly.

The key concept of this UR Aggro deck is to maintain tempo and pressure on the opponent by deploying cheap and efficient threats while disrupting their game plan with cards like Boomerang Basics and Lava Dart. The deck's low curve allows it to quickly establish board presence and apply pressure with Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler, supported by card selection from Preordain and Expressive Iteration. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently close out the game by combining burn spells like Lightning Bolt with evasive threats and prowess triggers.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize aggressive creatures while taking advantage of blink effects to protect them, generate value, and disrupt your opponent's game plan. By blinking creatures such as Ocelot Pride, Quantum Riddler, and Guide of Souls with cards like Ephemerate and Solitude, you can generate multiple triggers, protect your creatures, and control the board. Consign to Memory and Prismatic Ending provide removal options, while Ranger-Captain of Eos can tutor for key pieces. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficiently costed creatures and disruptive interaction.

This deck revolves around utilizing Broodscale Bloodchief's ability to create a lethal combo with Glaring Fleshraker and Basking Broodscale. By generating mana with Urza's Saga and Eldrazi Temple, players can assemble the combo quickly and overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures. Supporting cards like Malevolent Rumble, Unholy Heat, and Ancient Stirrings help control the board and find crucial pieces for the combo. The deck is designed to be fast and efficient, aiming to win the game before opponents can react effectively.

The main strategy of this deck revolves around using cards like Creeping Chill, Faithless Looting, and Burning Inquiry to fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Arclight Phoenix and Golgari Thug. By utilizing cards like Life from the Loam and Otherworldly Gaze to dredge cards from the graveyard, players can quickly bring back these creatures to the battlefield and overwhelm their opponent with a fast and aggressive combo. The deck also includes burn spells like Lava Dart and Conflagrate to deal additional damage and maintain board control.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature attacks combined with burn spells and disruptive elements to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The key strategy is to establish board presence early with efficient creatures like Ragavan and Guide of Souls, while using cards like Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge to clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to provide additional threats and utility. By applying pressure and disrupting the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on applying early pressure with efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, backed up by burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge. It also utilizes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer to generate additional value and card advantage. The inclusion of Goblin Bombardment provides another angle of attack and reach to finish off opponents. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently, leveraging its aggressive creatures and versatile spells to secure victories in the early turns of the game.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is focused on disrupting the opponent's mana base and resource management through land destruction and removal spells. The deck includes a variety of cards that can either destroy opponent's lands or creatures, such as Cleansing Wildfire, Field of Ruin, and Wrath of the Skies. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Price of Freedom and Reprieve to provide protection and recursion for key creatures. With a mix of control elements and disruptive strategies, this deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent in order to secure victory.

The Boros Aggro deck focuses on creating a fast and aggressive tempo by utilizing efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, backed up with burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Galvanic Discharge for reach. The deck also includes cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Goblin Bombardment for additional value and board presence. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Voice of Victory provide anthem effects to pump up the team, while Ranger-Captain of Eos helps with tutoring for key creatures or removal spells. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently with a mix of creatures and burn spells.

The key strategy of this Jeskai Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan by countering their spells, removing their threats, and controlling the game with powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The deck also utilizes various tools like Isochron Scepter, Traumatic Critique, and Tune the Narrative to further control the battlefield. By maintaining control of the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying aggressive creatures and controlling the board with removal spells. The key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Scion of Draco, backed up by Leyline of the Guildpact to boost their power. Consign to Memory provides additional removal and card advantage, while Leyline Binding helps to lock down key threats. Stubborn Denial offers protection against removal spells, allowing the deck to maintain pressure on the opponent. The mana base is optimized for consistency and speed, ensuring the deck can execute its game plan effectively.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays while utilizing the "blink" mechanic to temporarily remove threats or generate value from enter-the-battlefield effects. It combines efficient removal spells like Fatal Push with powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk. The deck also includes cards like Flickerwisp and Ephemerate to repeatedly blink and reuse key cards. The inclusion of cards like Thoughtseize and Recruiter of the Guard allows for disruption and tutoring, adding consistency to the deck's gameplan of aggressive attacking and blinking creatures for value.

The main focus of this Dimir Control deck is to dominate the battlefield through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures. By utilizing a mix of hand disruption, efficient removal, and countermagic, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and limit the opponent's options. The inclusion of powerful threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, as well as versatile cards like Cling to Dust and Drown in the Loch, ensures that the Dimir Control player can adapt to various situations and maintain a dominant position throughout the match.

Utilizing land ramp and untapping effects, the deck aims to quickly ramp up to play Primeval Titan or Cultivator Colossus, allowing for explosive plays with Scapeshift to search for lands such as Boseiju, Who Endures or The Mycosynth Gardens to further enhance the combo potential. The deck focuses on creating a strong board presence and maximizing the use of Amulet of Vigor to ensure efficient mana usage. The inclusion of cards like Zuran Orb and Aftermath Analyst provide additional utility and synergy within the combo-heavy strategy.

This UW Control deck focuses on disruption and removal to control the board and counter opponent's spells. With a balance of counterspells, removal spells, creatures, and planeswalkers, the deck aims to disrupt opponent's game plan while generating card advantage. The deck also includes utility lands to further control opponent's resources. By managing the board and hand, the deck seeks to out-value the opponent and ultimately win the game through card advantage and control.

The key strategy of this deck revolves around utilizing the UrzaTron lands to generate massive amounts of mana, allowing for the casting of powerful Eldrazi creatures and other spells to control the game. Cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Spirit Dragon provide versatile removal and lockdown effects, while Thought-Knot Seer disrupts opponent's strategies. Emrakul, the Promised End acts as a game-ending threat, and cards like All Is Dust and Trinisphere hinder opponent's board development. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents through a combination of powerful threats and disruptive spells.
